note: Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/CrossProduct.lean:110-114 — theorem cross_dot_cross (u v w x : Fin 3 → R): u⨯₃v ⬝ᵥ w⨯₃x = u⬝ᵥw*v⬝ᵥx - u⬝ᵥx*v⬝ᵥw, docstring 'the scalar quadruple product identity, related to the Binet-Cauchy identity'. Setting w=u,x=v recovers Lagrange's identity |u×v|²=|u|²|v|²-(u·v)² (used exactly this way in Mathlib/Geometry/Euclidean/Angle/Unoriented/CrossProduct.lean:32-37 as an inline step, not a standalone lemma); the cited decl is the strictly more general 4-argument Binet-Cauchy form, and only in R^3 (not the general n-dim sum-over-pairs identity).
verify_note: Wikidata Q1206853 + en.wikipedia.org/Lagrange%27s_identity confirm the concept's primary/lead statement IS the general n-dim sum-over-pairs identity, with the R^3 cross-product form explicitly presented as only a 'specialized case'; cross_dot_cross is hardcoded to Fin 3 (mathlib's cross product only exists in R^3) so it can never cover n≠3 — it generalizes only the R^3 special case along an orthogonal (2-arg→4-arg) axis, it does not generalize the concept itself. 'generalization' implies the decl subsumes the concept the way Module fully subsumes Vector space (docs/BRAIN-API.md:20-21); here the concept's canonical general form is entirely uncovered, so this is the same overclaim pattern one grade down from exact — the honest label is 'related'.

note: Mathlib/Logic/Relation.lean:896-897 — theorem church_rosser (h : ∀ a b c, r a b → r a c → ∃ d, ReflGen r b d ∧ ReflTransGen r c d) ...: Join (ReflTransGen r) b c, docstring 'A sufficient condition for the Church-Rosser property'. Mathlib's own docstring at GroupTheory/FreeGroup/Basic.lean:34-35 names the specialized FreeGroup.Red.church_rosser 'also known as Newman's diamond lemma' (repeated in the file's Tags). Caveat: the hypothesis used is one-step strong local confluence (no well-foundedness/termination premise), not the classical Newman's-Lemma hypothesis of local confluence + termination on a general ARS — same topic, distinct sufficient condition, so graded related rather than exact.
verify_note: decl exists and source/docstrings match verbatim (Relation.lean:896-897, FreeGroup/Basic.lean:34-35+53+191-193 all confirmed, incl. the explicit 'also known as Newman's diamond lemma' cross-reference); Wikidata Q1208706 confirms label 'Newman's lemma' aka diamond lemma; the noted hypothesis mismatch (strong one-step confluence vs. classical local-confluence+termination) is real and correctly downgrades this from exact to related — an honest, non-overclaiming grade.

note: Mathlib/Combinatorics/SimpleGraph/Basic.lean:150-152 — def completeBipartiteGraph (V W : Type*) : SimpleGraph (V ⊕ W) where Adj v w := v.isLeft ∧ w.isRight ∨ v.isRight ∧ w.isLeft. The utility graph K_{3,3} is exactly completeBipartiteGraph (Fin 3) (Fin 3); Mathlib only has the fully generic V,W construction, no named K_{3,3}/utility-graph instance.
verify_note: decl exists and source matches (def at line 152, docstring 148-150); Wikidata Q123375397 confirms label 'utility graph' = K_{3,3} = 'complete bipartite graph with 3 vertices in each independent set', exactly matching the claim; repo-wide grep for K_{3,3}/utility-graph/K33/Thomsen finds no dedicated mathlib def, and instantiating the generic decl at V=W=Fin 3 recovers the concept completely and exactly (same total-subsumption pattern as Module/Vector-space in docs/BRAIN-API.md:20-21) — a clean, honest generalization grade.
